task: I’ve got a claim and a few letters back and forth, and it’s starting to feel like it could sit there unless someone keeps track of it. Can you help me think through a simple way to stay organized and know what to send or note next?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the easiest way is to keep **one simple claim log** and a **document folder** so nothing gets lost and you always know the next step.

## 1) Keep one master log
Use a notebook, spreadsheet, or notes app. A simple one-page log works well.

**Columns to use:**
- **Date**
- **Who/Company**
- **What was received or said**
- **What you sent/did**
- **Proof it was sent**  
  (email sent copy, portal screenshot, certified mail receipt, tracking number, etc.)
- **Money paid out / amount**
- **Next step / deadline**

## 2) Save every document
Keep these together in one place:
- The **notice/claim letter**
- The **envelope** or **full email header**
- **Date received**
- **Claim/reference number**
- Every **letter, text, email, and portal message**
- Notes from any **phone calls**  
  Include **date, time, name of person, and what was said**
- Any **photos, receipts, estimates, or invoices**
- Proof of anything you **sent back**

## 3) After every contact, write 4 things
Right after a call, email, or letter, note:
1. **What happened**
2. **What they asked for**
3. **What you need to send or do**
4. **The deadline**

## 4) Always track the next move
At the end of each entry, ask:
- **Did I already send this?**
- **Do I have proof?**
- **What is the deadline?**
- **Who should follow up if I don’t hear back?**

## 5) Use a “pending” list
If something is waiting on them, put it on a short list:
- Sent on:
- Waiting for:
- Follow-up date:
- Deadline:

If you want, I can make you a **very simple one-page claim tracker template** you can copy and use right away.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. I’d use it. But make it tighter — one page, not some fancy workbook.

What I’d want next is a plain template with:
- date
- who it went to
- what they asked for
- what I sent
- proof of sending
- deadline
- next follow-up date

And I’d want a separate little section for phone calls, because those get slippery fast. Name, time, what was said. That’s it.

If you can give me a bare-bones version I can copy into a notebook, that’ll do.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a bare-bones one-page version you can copy into a notebook:

## Claim Log

| Date | Who it went to | What they asked for | What I sent | Proof of sending | Deadline | Next follow-up date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

## Phone Calls

| Date | Time | Name | What was said |
|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

## Quick notes
- Keep every letter/email in one folder.
- Write the date on anything you receive.
- If you send something, note how you sent it and keep proof.

If you want, I can also make this into an even tighter **plain text version** with no table lines, so it’s easier to handwrite.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s fine. Plain enough.

I’d use the notebook version, not the table junk if I’m writing by hand, but the content’s right. Date, who, what they wanted, what I sent, proof, deadline, follow-up. Phone calls separate. Good.

That’s all I needed.
